config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: SAYA TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, hak individu harus tetap menjadi batas utama agar kepentingan masyarakat tidak berubah menjadi alasan untuk menekan kebebasan, martabat, atau keselamatan orang per orang. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi seharusnya dicapai lewat keseimbangan dan perlindungan hak, bukan dengan mengorbankan individu secara mudah. Dalam pandangan saya, masyarakat yang sehat justru dibangun dari warga yang haknya dihormati.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society's interests should automatically take priority over individual rights. From my American perspective, that kind of rule is dangerous because it can become an excuse for government or the majority to trample dissent, privacy, and minority protections in the name of the "greater good." At the same time, individual rights are not absolute in every situation, since public safety and basic order do matter, but the burden should be on society to justify any limit very carefully. In general, a free society works best when rights are the default and collective interests are the exception, not the other way around.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tidak setuju jika kepentingan masyarakat dijadikan prioritas mutlak atas hak individu. Sebagai orang Indonesia, saya melihat terlalu sering alasan “demi kepentingan umum” dipakai untuk membenarkan pembatasan yang berlebihan, padahal yang seharusnya terjadi adalah pembatasan yang sangat terukur dan diawasi ketat. Hak individu memang tidak absolut, terutama saat ada keselamatan publik atau krisis, tetapi beban pembenarannya harus berat dan tidak boleh jadi pembenaran untuk menekan kelompok kecil atau suara yang berbeda. Menurut saya, masyarakat yang kuat justru dibangun dari perlindungan hak warga, bukan dari kebiasaan mengorbankan individu demi mayoritas.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should not take automatic priority over individual rights, because that is exactly how majorities justify abusing minorities and dissenters. From an American perspective, I am very wary of any argument that starts with "the common good" and ends with the government or the crowd deciding whose freedom matters less. That said, I do not believe rights are absolute in every case; public safety, emergencies, and preventing real harm can justify limits, but those limits need to be narrow, evidence-based, and accountable. In my view, a healthy society protects the individual first and then proves, very carefully, when a collective exception is truly necessary.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Kepentingan masyarakat tidak boleh menjadi prioritas mutlak atas hak individu. Sebagai orang Indonesia, saya justru curiga kalau frasa “demi kepentingan umum” dipakai terlalu mudah, karena sering sekali itu berubah jadi alat untuk menekan kritik, kelompok kecil, atau orang yang tidak punya kuasa. Hak individu memang bisa dibatasi dalam situasi tertentu seperti keselamatan publik atau keadaan darurat, tetapi pembatasannya harus ketat, jelas, dan bisa dipertanggungjawabkan. Kalau tidak, yang disebut kepentingan masyarakat hanya akan jadi alasan yang rapi untuk ketidakadilan.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should take priority over individual rights as a general rule. From my American perspective, that idea is too easy to weaponize: once you say the collective comes first, it becomes much simpler for governments or majorities to silence unpopular people and call it "for the common good." I do think there are real cases, like emergencies or public safety, where limited restrictions are justified, but they should be narrow, evidence-based, and open to challenge. In a free society, the burden should be on society to prove why an individual right must be limited, not on the individual to justify why their freedom deserves protection.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
